risk_title: NGO Consortium Withdrawal Affecting OOSC Implementation Capacity
risk_summary: Save the Children led NGO Consortium has withdrawn from the project, forcing MBSSE to assume direct responsibility for supporting 120,000 out-of-school children. This represents a significant shift in implementation arrangements and raises questions about MBSSE's institutional capacity to manage this expanded mandate.
evidence_quote: As the Save the Children led NGO Consortium has withdrawn from the project, MBSSE will take up their assigned activities and support all 120,000 OOSC.

risk_title: Delays in School-Related Gender-Based Violence (SRGBV) Reporting
risk_summary: The mission noted delays in reporting of school-related gender-based violence cases, indicating potential gaps in the protection and safeguarding systems for students. This affects the project's ability to respond to violence incidents affecting vulnerable populations.
evidence_quote: The mission noted delays in reporting of some school-related gender-based violence (SRGBV) cases, and stressed the importance of timely reporting of cases, ensuring easily accessible reporting modalities, to enhance timely reporting

risk_title: Teacher Perpetrators Absconding from Law Enforcement
risk_summary: Teachers accused of SRGBV are absconding from the law, indicating governance and rule-of-law challenges in holding perpetrators accountable. This undermines child protection objectives and suggests weak coordination between education and justice systems.
evidence_quote: The MBSSE has confirmed that teachers accused of SRGBV who are absconding from the law, can be immediately blacklisted.

risk_title: Sexual Harassment and Drug Abuse on School Transport
risk_summary: Sexual harassment and drug abuse incidents have been identified on school buses in Freetown and Bo, indicating safety and security challenges affecting student protection in urban areas. This requires coordination with local authorities and transport operators.
evidence_quote: The mission discussed the issue of sexual harassment and drug abuse on school buses in Freetown and Bo. It was agreed that the MBSSE leadership would arrange a meeting with the local council and the Sierra Leone Road Transport Corporation to put in place measures to secure safe school bus travel.

risk_title: Coordination Gaps Between Education and Justice Systems
risk_summary: The project requires coordination between MBSSE, TSC, Attorney General, and other justice sector actors to address SRGBV. The need for a newly designated senior prosecutor focal point and development of sanctions frameworks suggests previously weak institutional linkages.
evidence_quote: The mission and the MBSSE met with the Attorney General (AG) on February 27, 2025. It was agreed that the necessary legal support will be provided, and a senior prosecutor was designated as the focal point for providing legal advice to the MBSSE/TSC.

risk_title: Weak Teacher Code of Conduct Enforcement Framework
risk_summary: TSC has expressed the need to develop a sanctions framework relating to violations of the Teacher Code of Conduct, indicating that enforcement mechanisms for teacher misconduct are currently inadequate, affecting child protection in schools.
evidence_quote: TSC has expressed the need to develop a sanctions framework relating to violations of the Teacher Code of Conduct, and these regulations/guidelines are to be approved by the TSC board and the MBSSE management.
